title: PatchFeatures Object
description: Collection that provides access to all of the existing Patch features in a component and supports the ability to create new Patch features.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates a PatchFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on the returned PatchFeatureInput object to set other settings. The PatchFeatureInput object is used as input to the add method to create the patch feature.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified patch feature using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified patch feature using the name of the feature.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of Patch features in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Patch Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new patch feature.

add.title: PatchFeatures.add Method
add.return_description: Returns the newly created PatchFeature object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A PatchFeatureInput object that defines the desired patch feature. Use the createInput method to create a new PatchFeatureInput object and then use methods on it (the PatchFeatureInput object) to define the patch feature.

related_samples.name: patchFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the patchFeatures.add method by creating a patch surface on the selected profile.

related_samples.name: Patch Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new patch feature.

classType.title: PatchFeatures.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: PatchFeatures.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates a PatchFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on the returned PatchFeatureInput object to set other settings. The PatchFeatureInput object is used as input to the add method to create the patch feature.
createInput.return_description: Returns the newly created PatchFeatureInput object or null if the creation fails.

parameters.name: boundaryCurve
parameters.description: Defines the input geometry that will be used to define the boundary. This can be a sketch profile, a single sketch curve, a single B-Rep edge, an ObjectCollection, or a Path object.   If a single sketch curve or B-Rep edge is an input that is not closed; Fusion will automatically find connected sketch curves or B-Rep edges to define a closed loop.   If an ObjectCollection is an input, it must be a set of curves that define a closed shape.   If a Path is an input, it must define a closed shape.

parameters.name: operation
parameters.description: The feature operation to perform. Only 'NewBodyFeatureOperation' and 'NewComponentFeatureOperation' are valid operations for patch features.

related_samples.name: patchFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the patchFeatures.add method by creating a patch surface on the selected profile.

related_samples.name: Patch Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new patch feature.

item.title: PatchFeatures.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified patch feature using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: PatchFeatures.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified patch feature using the name of the feature.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if the specified name was not found.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the feature within the collection to return. This is the name seen in the timeline.

count.title: PatchFeatures.count Property
count.description: The number of Patch features in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = patchFeatures_var.count

isValid.title: PatchFeatures.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = patchFeatures_var.isValid

objectType.title: PatchFeatures.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:

propertyValue = patchFeatures_var.objectType
